greeting: Week in review. This was a week of strategic pivots and operational momentum — the USCIS Expedite Request project was paused by leadership, Detention Prevention kicked off its Wave 2 client outreach, and the CoE backlog prioritization continued to drive how the team allocates effort. Several key scoping calls and cross-functional syncs kept the project machine moving, even with Jhara out all week.

items.label: Detention Prevention Wave 2 launched
items.detail: Santiago and team finalized the new survey and client statement, then kicked off Wave 2 of client outreach. Nearly completed the forecasted client contacts by end of week. Strong execution while Jhara was OOO.

items.label: Firmwide Critical Dates taxonomy presented
items.detail: Javier Vargas led the meeting on the new taxonomy for deadlines, notices, and reminders. PPT and recording shared firmwide — this standardization has been a long time coming.

items.label: Salesforce Roadmap 2026 v2 shared
items.detail: Joshua Johanni shared the updated LLF Salesforce Roadmap 2026 v2 with the team. This gives the PMO clearer visibility into the IT/Litify trajectory for the year.

items.label: Executive project status deck process improved
items.detail: Yuri and Jean coordinated on the weekly executive slide update, including marking stalled projects honestly (red). Conor and Mike contributed staff augmentation updates. Transparency over optics.

items.label: USCIS Expedite Request project temporarily paused
items.detail: Leadership decided to pause the UER project to align with broader strategic priorities. Yuri communicated the pause on Monday. Project marked as stalled/red on the executive deck — not hidden, not sugarcoated.

items.detail: Per Jill's direction via Shelley, the Denial/RFE Reason dropdown update will be handled as a standalone IT ticket through Josh rather than staying bundled in the larger CoE project. Shelley flagged this as very urgent.

items.label: Detention Prevention survey keeps service name as open question
items.detail: Team decided to keep the service name as an open question in the client survey, with a closing question asking clients to suggest a name. Collaborative approach to branding.

items.label: Denial/RFE dropdown update still pending
items.detail: Shelley marked this as very urgent on Friday. Josh needs to submit the IT ticket to update the dropdown. Jean owns the follow-up to ensure this gets actioned Monday — it's been lingering in the CoE backlog and needs to move independently now.

items.detail: Urgent client escalation flagged Friday involving Alejandro, Katheryn, and Zulay. Needs attention and resolution tracking into next week.
